text: When working on a software project, Git branching is a key part of the version control workflow. Developers create feature branches to work on new ideas or fixes separately from the main codebase. This keeps the main branch stable and allows multiple people to work at the same time. Once a feature is complete, it is usually shared through a pull request. Other team members can review the changes, suggest improvements, and discuss the code. After approval, the branch is merged into the main branch. This process helps teams track changes, avoid conflicts, and ensure that only high-quality code is added to the project.
